source_metadata.title_raw: R. Brent Lemon - A-18-146 - September 10, 2018 - Elk Grove

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 10, 2018

R. Brent Lemon
5801 Laguna Vale Way
Elk Grove, CA 95758

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-146

Dear Mr. Lemon:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding post-governmental employment
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are not a finder of fact
when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes
your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions
should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

Given that you did not work for or represent any other state administrative agency in the 12
months prior to permanently leaving the California High-Speed Rail Authority (the "Authority"),
does the Act's "One-Year Ban" apply to you with respect to your potential participation in a
California Department of Transportation ("Caltrans") contract for professional services as an
independent contractor?

CONCLUSION

No. The One-Year Ban does not apply to you with respect to Caltrans and its officers or
employees so long as Caltrans' budget, personnel, and other operations are not subject to the
Authority's "direction and control" as described in Regulation 18746.1(b)(6)(C)(ii).

FACTS

You are a former state employee who has been employed by two separate state agencies in
the past, Caltrans and the Authority. You were a Supervising Transportation Engineer at Caltrans, a
position designated in Caltrans' Conflict-of-Interest Code, when you resigned from Caltrans in
March of 2005.

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-146
Page No. 2

You were employed as a Supervising Transportation Engineer at the Authority, a position
designated in the Authority's Conflict-of-Interest Code, from November of 2016 through April of
2018, when you retired from state service. You state that you did not work for or represent any
other state agency while employed by the Authority.

On August 16, 2018, you further advised that you may seek to pursue Caltrans' professional
service contracts as an independent contractor. These contracts are completely unrelated to the
Authority or high-speed rail in general.

ANALYSIS

The One-Year Ban prohibits a former designated employee of a state administrative agency,
among others, from representing any other person for compensation by appearing before or
communicating with any state administrative agency that the former employee worked for or
represented during the 12 months prior to leaving state service, or any officer or employee of that
agency, for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action or any action or
proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, award, or revocation of a permit, license, grant,
contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property. (Section 87406.)

A former designated employee of a state administrative agency is also subject to the One-
Year Ban with respect to any other state administrative agency whose budget, personnel, and other
operations are subject to the direction and control of the former employee's former agency, and that
other agency's officers or employees. (Regulation 18746.1(b)(6)(C)(ii).) However, "whether an
agency is provided technical assistance or legal advice, or is subject to oversight, by another agency
pursuant to state law, are not factors to be considered in determining whether an agency is subject
to the direction and control of another." (Ibid.)

Based on the facts presented, you did not work for or represent any other state
administrative agency in the 12 months prior to permanently leaving the Authority. Therefore, you
are not subject to the One-Year Ban with respect to Caltrans and its officers and employees unless
Caltrans is subject to the "direction and control" of the Authority as described in Regulation
18746.1(b)(6)(C)(i).

You have provided no facts to provide support for a determination that Caltrans is subject to
the requisite "direction and control" of the Authority under that regulation for the One-Year Ban to
also apply to you with respect to Caltrans and its officers and employees. We note that Public
Utilities Code Section 185037 provides that the Authority may contract with Caltrans to perform
project design and engineering services, including construction services, for any project along the
high-speed rail network. This alone, however, does not provide a basis for us to conclusively
determine whether Caltrans is subject to the Authority's "direction and control" as described in
Regulation 18746.1(b)(6)(C)(ii).

Thus, based upon the facts you have provided, we conclude that the One-Year Ban does not
apply to you with respect to Caltrans and its officers and employees so long as Caltrans' budget,
personnel, and other operations are not subject to the Authority's direction and control as described
in Regulation 18746.1(b)(6)(C)(ii).

File No. A-18-146
Page No. 3

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Acting General Counsel

By: Matthew F. Christy
Counsel, Legal Division

MFC:jgl

sections.question:

independent contractor?

No. The One-Year Ban does not apply to you with respect to Caltrans and its officers or 
employees so long as Caltrans' budget, personnel, and other operations are not subject to the 
Authority's "direction and control" as described in Regulation 18746.l(b)(6)(C)(ii).
